fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kansas Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Kansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity kansas, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to kansas, pharmacy technician compact kansas, kansas pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Kansas Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)
board.mailing_address:
Kansas Board of Pharmacy
800 SW Jackson, Suite 1414
Topeka, KS 66612-1244

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Kansas.

steps.title: 1. Create Account & Submit Online Application
steps.description: Navigate to the Kansas Board of Pharmacy Online Licensing Portal (kansas.gov/bop), select 'Apply for a New License,' then 'Pharmacy Technician.' Create an account, complete all required fields, and submit the application.

steps.title: 2. Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $20.00 directly through the online licensing portal during the application submission process.

steps.title: 3. Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Obtain two fingerprint cards (available from the Board or local law enforcement), complete them according to the provided instructions (pharmacy.ks.gov/documents/Fingerprint%20Instructions.pdf), and mail them to the Kansas Board of Pharmacy for processing.

steps.title: 4. Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload or mail any additional required documentation, such as proof of age (e.g., driver's license) and proof of high school diploma/GED or enrollment in a training program, as specified in the online application.

steps.title: 5. Await Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Kansas Board of Pharmacy will review your complete application, background check results, and all supporting documentation. Upon successful verification of all requirements, your Pharmacy Technician license will be issued and viewable via the online license search.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Kansas offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Kansas Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Kansas by submitting a new application to the Kansas Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Kansas-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Kansas pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Kansas have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Kansas. To work in Kansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Kansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Kansas by submitting a new application to the Kansas Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Kansas-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.
